text: When working on a software project, using Git for version control allows multiple people to collaborate efficiently. Instead of editing the main code directly, developers create feature branches. These are separate copies where they can build and test new functionality without affecting the stable version. Once a feature is complete, they submit a pull request, which lets team members review the changes, discuss potential issues, and approve or request modifications. After approval, the changes are merged back into the main branch. This workflow keeps the project organised, reduces conflicts, and ensures that only well-reviewed code becomes part of the final product.
